title: When More Means Less: Understanding The Paradox of Choice

questions.sentence: In today's consumer-driven world, we are constantly presented with an abundance of options.
Intuitively, we assume that more choices lead to greater freedom, happiness, and a higher chance of finding exactly what we want.
Schwartz posits that while a certain amount of choice is liberating, an excessive amount can actually be detrimental to our well-being.
Faced with an overwhelming array of possibilities, individuals may find it so difficult to make a selection that they end up making no choice at all, or postponing it indefinitely.
When we choose one option from many, we are more likely to experience 'buyer's remorse' and wonder if we made the optimal decision.
Ultimately, by recognising that boundless choice can hinder rather than help, we can cultivate a more mindful approach to decision-making and, paradoxically, find greater satisfaction in our lives.
